response:
The `/decide` process ran to completion: opposing advisor panel → PM decision → red-team attack (which found a fatal contradiction in the initial draft) → PM revision folding in all four breaks → independent verifier pass (all PASS, no blockers). Here's the final decision record, saved at `docs/decisions/0001-v1-geocoding-api-sunset.md`:

---

```
---
id: 0001
title: Sunset the v1 geocoding API
status: Accepted
date: 2026-07-14
decider: PM (accountable)
revised: 2026-07-14 (red-team breaks 1-4 folded in)
summary: Firm-pending v1 sunset (2027-01-14, contingent on the T-30 tripwire) for the silent 0.4% tail; PM-pushed per-account extensions (cap 2027-07-14) for the three June accounts; shim demoted to a conditional track gated on a v1/v2 equivalence audit.
tags: [contested, checkable, reframe, contradiction]
supersedes: none
---

# ADR 0001 - Sunset the v1 geocoding API

## Weakest assumption (most load-bearing)
- [contradiction] We do NOT know whether v2 returns the same geocoding results
  as v1 for the same inputs. The memo's "every bugfix written and tested twice"
  is behavioral divergence, not just shape. So a v2-backed shim matching v1's
  shape can still silently change the coordinates NorthRoute's live maps show and
  the routes Ferrymark's nightly batch produces. Every claim below about the shim
  "serving the v1 contract" is downstream of a result-equivalence audit not yet
  run. Until it passes, no "no behavior change" promise is made.
  REOPEN-IF the audit shows material v1/v2 result drift.

## Context
v1 (original) and v2 (14 months old). v1 is 0.4% of traffic (~2,900 req/day;
version_traffic.csv, 14-day window, flat with no decline - evidence of live
automated integrations, not abandonment). Company cost: the paths drifted far
enough that every bugfix is written and tested twice - direct evidence the
engines behave differently. June complaints (complaints.md): NorthRoute (06-08)
parses v1's flat lat/lng/label in live dispatcher maps - stated pain is shape,
but value drift hits them silently; Ferrymark (06-14) v1 load-bearing for a
nightly routing workflow, "no time to touch it"; Tavo (06-11) 2-person shop,
"no engineering bandwidth this year."

## Decision
Option A variant - firm-pending sunset of the v1 contract with PM-pushed,
per-account carve-outs. The v2-backed v1-shape shim is a conditional track, not
the headline: it earns customer commitments only if scoped cheap AND proven
result-equivalent.

Reframe (holds, weaker): company pain is the divergent code path; customer pain
is shape/workflow stability - separable. But the shim does not automatically
dissolve customer pain: it serves v1's shape for free and its values only if
v2 agrees with v1. End state - one engine + one thin adapter - is a goal
contingent on the audit, not a this-quarter fact.

Commitments:
1. Push, not pull. The three June emails count as engagement already - no
   account re-replies to qualify. PM-initiated scoping starts now: per-account
   migration cost AND the v1/v2 equivalence audit.
2. Firm-pending sunset for the silent tail: 2027-01-14 (T+184d) - "pending"
   because explicitly contingent on the T-30 tripwire (item 5), which, given
   flat traffic that never reads email, is likely to fire. Not an unconditional
   date.
3. Notices - 4 emails to every identifiable v1 caller (T-184/T-90/T-30/T-7);
   plus RFC 8594 Sunset+Deprecation headers on every v1 response now.
4. Shim = conditional track. Customer-facing only if BOTH (a) eng scopes it
   clearly cheaper than the migrations it replaces AND (b) the audit passes (or
   each affected customer accepts the delta in writing). Until then it is R&D;
   its cost is UP front (runs both engines plus the adapter before v1's engine
   can be deleted), and steady state is one engine plus a permanently-maintained
   adapter, not literally "one engine."
5. Tripwire (review, not auto-extension): at T-30, if v1 is still >~1,000
   req/day from uncontacted callers, hold a go/no-go first (silence != death).

The three named accounts (REOPEN per-account, not all-or-nothing): white-glove
help + cost assessment; extensions to a hard cap of 2027-07-14 sized to scoped
cost, never offered to the silent tail. NorthRoute & Ferrymark (live dispatch /
nightly routing) get an extension by default to the cap unless/until the shim
is proven equivalent for their calls - not asked to "touch nothing" on an
unaudited engine swap. Tavo gets an extension covering their no-bandwidth year;
the shim, if later proven, can absorb them, but their carve-out does not depend
on it.

## Rationale vs alternatives
- Vs Advisor 2 (no date, usage floor): flat traffic sits at ~6x the ~500/day
  floor, so a floor-only gate may never fire (indefinite dual-maint). Keep its
  best move (get cost + equivalence data first) via per-account carve-outs and
  tripwire, without losing the clock.
- Vs Advisor 1 (60d + 90d carve): 60 days can't scope then execute an un-costed
  rewrite plus live QA. Keep the forcing function at 6 months; scope cost first.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] A shim over v2 is weeks not months - speculation; no codebase
  here. Not load-bearing: if false or the audit fails, fall to real per-account
  migration on the same calendar.
- [checkable] Dual-maintenance cost is real/non-trivial; if trivial, urgency
  drops but the date stands.
- [checkable] ~2,900/day is meaningfully flat, not a 14-day artifact - verify
  over a longer window before T-30.
- [judgment] The silent tail mixes abandoned and live-but-quiet integrations;
  flatness leans live - hence headers + tripwire, not a blind cut.

## What would change my mind / monitor
- REOPEN-IF the audit shows material drift -> shim can't serve the contract;
  NorthRoute/Ferrymark stay on real migrations to the cap.
- REOPEN-IF the shim is scoped in months -> drop that track, keep the date.
- REOPEN-IF any one named account is scoped in months -> extend that account to
  the cap (per-account, so a resourced NorthRoute reply can't strand
  Tavo/Ferrymark).
- REOPEN-IF a new large v1 integration appears or traffic rises -> thin-tail
  breaks.
- Monitor weekly: req/day trend, per-account progress, audit status,
  uncontacted callers pre-T-30.
```

---

Verifier confirmed the traffic math (0.4%, ~2,940 req/day), all dates, and internal consistency all check out — no blockers. I also saved two memory notes for future sessions in this project: how to run `/decide` manually now that it's blocked from direct Skill-tool invocation, and a summary of this decision.
